description: CSS prototype pollution / CSS custom-property injection — attacker-controlled CSS custom property (--var) leaks data via @import, attribute selectors, or background-image: url() exfiltration.

custom_property_injection.description: Stored or reflected CSS custom property where attacker controls --variable values that downstream rules consume.

probes.description: Inject background-image with Collaborator URL. Replace COLLABORATOR with output of generate_collaborator_payload().
@import injection — exfiltrates the page rendering context to attacker via Collaborator stylesheet load.
Attribute-selector exfil — stage 1 of CSS-only password / token exfil. Each prefix character triggers different background URL.

style_attribute_injection.description: Reflected style attribute (style="...") where attacker controls a value used in url(), expression(), or behavior() (legacy IE).

probes.description: Break out of style attribute value into a new declaration. Collaborator hit confirms CSS execution.
Legacy Firefox/Mozilla XBL binding — gone in modern browsers but still served to old clients.

craft_guidance.when_filters_partial: If '<' filtered but ':' allowed: try CSS custom properties only. If 'url(' filtered: try @import without parens (rare). If both: only attribute-selector exfil works.
craft_guidance.when_no_collab: Use observable side-effects: ::before content based on selector, font-family with custom font URL, animation-name leak via DevTools timing.
craft_guidance.verification: Rule 9a: never use real domains. Use Collaborator subdomain or operator-provided callback (interact.sh, webhook.site). Replace COLLABORATOR placeholder at runtime.
